questions.question: What is a Promise in JavaScript/TypeScript?
options: A) A synchronous value wrapper
B) An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation
C) A callback function
D) A type of event listener

questions.question: What are the three states a Promise can be in?
options: A) open, closed, error
B) pending, fulfilled, rejected
C) waiting, success, failure
D) init, done, failed
A) undefined
B) Promise { 42 }
D) Error

questions.question: What does the 'async' keyword before a function do?
options: A) Makes the function run in a separate thread
B) Makes the function return a Promise automatically
C) Makes the function execute immediately
D) Adds error handling to the function

questions.question: What does 'await' do inside an async function?
options: A) Pauses the entire program
B) Pauses execution of the async function until the Promise settles
C) Cancels the Promise
D) Converts the Promise to a synchronous value globally
A) Promise { 'hello' }
B) undefined
C) 'hello'
D) Error: not a Promise

questions.question: What does Promise.all() do?
options: A) Runs promises one at a time in sequence
B) Waits for all promises to fulfill and returns an array of results; rejects if any reject
C) Returns the first resolved promise
D) Catches all errors from promises
C) [Promise, Promise, Promise]

questions.question: What happens with Promise.all() if one of the promises rejects?
options: A) It returns partial results
B) It waits for all to complete then reports errors
C) The entire Promise.all() rejects immediately with that error
D) It retries the failed promise

questions.question: What is the difference between Promise.all() and Promise.allSettled()?
options: A) No difference
B) allSettled waits for all promises to settle (fulfill or reject) and never short-circuits
C) allSettled only handles rejections
D) all is faster than allSettled

questions.question: What does Promise.race() return?
options: A) An array of all results
B) The result of the first promise to settle (fulfill or reject)
C) The fastest fulfilled promise only
D) A boolean indicating which promise won
A) undefined
B) 'oops'
C) 'caught: oops'
D) Unhandled promise rejection

questions.question: How do you handle errors in a .then() chain?
options: A) Use try/catch around the chain
B) Add a .catch() at the end of the chain
C) Use .error() method
D) Errors can't be caught in chains

questions.question: What does .finally() do on a Promise?
options: A) Executes only if the promise fulfills
B) Executes a callback regardless of whether the promise fulfilled or rejected
C) Converts the promise to a synchronous operation
D) Cancels the promise

questions.question: What is the return type of an async function that returns a number?
options: A) number
B) Promise<number>
C) async number
D) Awaited<number>

questions.question: What is a common retry pattern for async operations?
options: A) Using setInterval to repeat the call
B) A loop that awaits the operation, catches errors, and retries up to a max number of attempts
C) Calling the function recursively without limits
D) Using Promise.race with multiple copies of the same call
A) Calls fn once and throws on error
B) Calls fn up to 'attempts' times, returning on first success, throwing the last error if all fail
C) Calls fn infinitely until it succeeds
D) Runs fn in parallel 'attempts' times

questions.question: Can you use 'await' at the top level of a module in modern TypeScript?
options: A) No, await must always be inside an async function
B) Yes, if the module target supports top-level await (e.g., ES2022+)
C) Only in .mts files
D) Only with a special compiler flag
D) [Promise, Promise]

questions.question: Why is 'await' inside a loop sometimes a performance problem?
options: A) It causes memory leaks
B) Each iteration waits for the previous one, making operations sequential instead of concurrent
C) It blocks the event loop entirely
D) It creates too many Promises

questions.question: What does Promise.resolve(value) do?
options: A) Creates a rejected promise with the given value
B) Creates an immediately fulfilled promise with the given value
C) Waits for a value to resolve
D) Converts a callback to a promise

questions.question: What type does Promise.allSettled() return?
options: A) Promise<T[]>
B) Promise<PromiseSettledResult<T>[]>
C) Promise<(T | Error)[]>
D) Promise<{ fulfilled: T[]; rejected: Error[] }>
A) undefined
B) Error object
C) 'network error'
D) Unhandled rejection

questions.question: What is the Awaited<T> utility type used for?
options: A) Marking a type as async
B) Unwrapping the resolved type from a Promise<T> (recursively)
C) Creating a new Promise type
D) Converting sync types to async types
